column4: These are the generations of {{c1::Noah}}. Noah was in his generations a man righteous and wholehearted; Noah walked with God.
These are the {{c1::generations}} of Noah. Noah was in his generations a man righteous and wholehearted; Noah walked with God.
And {{c1::Noah}} begot three sons, Shem, Ham, and Japheth.
And Noah begot three sons, {{c1::Shem}}, Ham, and Japheth.
And the earth was {{c1::corrupt}} before God, and the earth was filled with violence.
And the earth was corrupt {{c1::before}} God, and the earth was filled with violence.
And God saw the earth, and, {{c1::behold}}, it was corrupt; for all flesh had corrupted their way upon the earth. .
And God saw the earth, and, behold, it was {{c1::corrupt}}; for all flesh had corrupted their way upon the earth. .
And God said unto {{c1::Noah}}: ‘The end of all flesh is come before Me; for the earth is filled with violence through them; and, behold, I will destroy them with the earth.
And God said unto Noah: ‘The end of all flesh is come before {{c1::Me}}; for the earth is filled with violence through them; and, behold, I will destroy them with the earth.
Make thee an ark of {{c1::gopher}} wood; with rooms shalt thou make the ark, and shalt pitch it within and without with pitch.
Make thee an ark of gopher wood; with rooms shalt thou make the ark, and shalt pitch it {{c1::within}} and without with pitch.
And this is how thou shalt make it: the {{c1::length}} of the ark three hundred cubits, the breadth of it fifty cubits, and the height of it thirty cubits.
And this is how thou shalt make it: the length of the ark three {{c1::hundred}} cubits, the breadth of it fifty cubits, and the height of it thirty cubits.

A light shalt thou make to the ark, and to a cubit shalt thou {{c1::finish}} it upward; and the door of the ark shalt thou set in the side thereof; with lower, second, and third stories shalt thou make it.

A light shalt thou make to the ark, and to a cubit shalt thou finish it {{c1::upward}}; and the door of the ark shalt thou set in the side thereof; with lower, second, and third stories shalt thou make it.
And {{c1::I}}, behold, I do bring the flood of waters upon the earth, to destroy all flesh, wherein is the breath of life, from under heaven; every thing that is in the earth shall perish.
And I, {{c1::behold}}, I do bring the flood of waters upon the earth, to destroy all flesh, wherein is the breath of life, from under heaven; every thing that is in the earth shall perish.
But {{c1::I}} will establish My covenant with thee; and thou shalt come into the ark, thou, and thy sons, and thy wife, and thy sons’ wives with thee.
But I will establish {{c1::My}} covenant with thee; and thou shalt come into the ark, thou, and thy sons, and thy wife, and thy sons’ wives with thee.
And of every {{c1::living}} thing of all flesh, two of every sort shalt thou bring into the ark, to keep them alive with thee; they shall be male and female.
And of every living thing of all flesh, two of every sort shalt thou bring into the ark, to keep them alive with thee; they shall be male and {{c1::female}}.
Of the fowl after their kind, and of the {{c1::cattle}} after their kind, of every creeping thing of the ground after its kind, two of every sort shall come unto thee, to keep them alive.
Of the fowl after their kind, and of the cattle after their kind, of every {{c1::creeping}} thing of the ground after its kind, two of every sort shall come unto thee, to keep them alive.
And take thou unto thee of all food that is eaten, and {{c1::gather}} it to thee; and it shall be for food for thee, and for them.’
Thus did {{c1::Noah}}; according to all that God commanded him, so did he.
Thus did Noah; {{c1::according}} to all that God commanded him, so did he.
And the LORD said unto {{c1::Noah}}: ‘Come thou and all thy house into the ark; for thee have I seen righteous before Me in this generation.
And the LORD said unto Noah: ‘{{c1::Come}} thou and all thy house into the ark; for thee have I seen righteous before Me in this generation.
Of every clean beast thou shalt take to thee seven and seven, each with his mate; and of the {{c1::beasts}} that are not clean two [and two], each with his mate;
of the fowl also of the air, seven and seven, male and {{c1::female}}; to keep seed alive upon the face of all the earth.
For yet seven days, and {{c1::I}} will cause it to rain upon the earth forty days and forty nights; and every living substance that I have made will I blot out from off the face of the earth.’

And {{c1::He}} blotted out every living substance which was upon the face of the ground, both man, and cattle, and creeping thing, and fowl of the heaven; and they were blotted out from the earth; and Noah only was left, and they that were with him in the ark.

And he sent forth a raven, and it went forth to and fro, until the {{c1::waters}} were dried up from off the earth.

And he sent forth a dove from him, to see if the waters were {{c1::abated}} from off the face of the ground.

But the dove found no rest for the sole of her foot, and she {{c1::returned}} unto him to the ark, for the waters were on the face of the whole earth; and he put forth his hand, and took her, and brought her in unto him into the ark.

And the LORD smelled the sweet savour; and the LORD said in His heart: ‘{{c1::I}} will not again curse the ground any more for man’s sake; for the imagination of man’s heart is evil from his youth; neither will I again smite any more every thing living, as I have done.

While the earth {{c1::remaineth}}, seedtime and harvest, and cold and heat, and summer and winter, and day and night shall not cease.’
While the earth remaineth, {{c1::seedtime}} and harvest, and cold and heat, and summer and winter, and day and night shall not cease.’

And the fear of you and the dread of you shall be upon every beast of the earth, and upon every fowl of the air, and upon all {{c1::wherewith}} the ground teemeth, and upon all the fishes of the sea: into your hand are they delivered.

Only flesh with the life {{c1::thereof}}, which is the blood thereof, shall ye not eat.

And surely your blood of your lives will {{c1::I}} require; at the hand of every beast will I require it; and at the hand of man, even at the hand of every man’s brother, will I require the life of man.
